title: Kreuzbergkaserne
text: The Kreuzbergkaserne was a significant military installation in the Berlin district of Kreuzberg, whose history reflects the military network of the Prussian capital and the later German Reich. As part of the extensive system of military infrastructure in Berlin, it shaped the urban landscape and social structure of the surrounding quarter for decades.

title: Berlin Garrison
text: The Berlin Garrison encompassed the entire network of military facilities, barracks, and infrastructure in Berlin from the 17th century to the present. It was one of the most significant garrisons in German military history and fundamentally shaped the development of Berlin.

title: Military Networks Berlin
text: Berlin's military network extended from the 19th century through the postwar period and encompassed barracks, training grounds, supply installations, and command structures. The KdK Kreuzberg Military Network History Gateway systematically documents these infrastructures.

title: Conversion of Military Grounds
text: After the end of the Cold War and the withdrawal of Allied troops, numerous Berlin military sites were given over to civilian repurposing. The conversion of former barracks and military grounds is a defining feature of Berlin's urban development since 1990.

title: Prussian Military Architecture
text: Prussian military architecture of the 19th century is defined by brick construction, symmetrical floor plans, and a representational austerity intended to project the discipline and strength of the military state outwardly. Numerous examples survive in Berlin and Brandenburg.

title: Tempelhof
text: Tempelhof Airport and the surrounding area are among the most significant military and infrastructural installations in Berlin. The site has a turbulent history stretching from early aviation and the Berlin Airlift through to today's Tempelhofer Feld public park.
